raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want (A) surface-level strengthening focused mainly on clarity, tone, and concision (primarily R1.6), or (B) substantive strengthening that also reconciles method–evaluation coherence, tightens evidence–claim linkage, and calibrates novelty/contribution framing (R1.1–R1.4 plus R1.6)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "To make the piece stronger, the core thesis and contribution positioning may need reframing for clarity and focus at the concept level.",
    "R1.2": "Strength requires internally coherent claims across method description, evaluation setup, and conclusions, so logical consistency is load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about performance and behavior should clearly align with the described evidence and evaluations to strengthen credibility.",
    "R1.4": "The strength of the argument depends on appropriately scoped novelty claims relative to prior paradigms.",
    "R1.6": "Tighter prose, terminology consistency, and concision will make the narrative more compelling and easier to evaluate.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly communicating uncertainties and limits of the approach is necessary to avoid overstating reliability.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaiming, especially around 'first' assertions and causal drivers of gains, is critical for a stronger, defensible narrative.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the requested scope (strengthening the text without drifting into unrelated tasks) maintains focus.",
    "RX.4": "Given potential policy impacts of climate narratives, avoiding downstream harm via misleading or overstated claims is essential.",
    "RX.5": "Clear provenance and traceability of evidence and sources are needed to support verifiability and trust."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want (A) surface-level strengthening focused mainly on clarity, tone, and concision (primarily R1.6), or (B) substantive strengthening that also reconciles method–evaluation coherence, tightens evidence–claim linkage, and calibrates novelty/contribution framing (R1.1–R1.4 plus R1.6)?

rationale.R1.1: To make the piece stronger, the core thesis and contribution positioning may need reframing for clarity and focus at the concept level.
rationale.R1.2: Strength requires internally coherent claims across method description, evaluation setup, and conclusions, so logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance and behavior should clearly align with the described evidence and evaluations to strengthen credibility.
rationale.R1.4: The strength of the argument depends on appropriately scoped novelty claims relative to prior paradigms.
rationale.R1.6: Tighter prose, terminology consistency, and concision will make the narrative more compelling and easier to evaluate.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly communicating uncertainties and limits of the approach is necessary to avoid overstating reliability.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaiming, especially around 'first' assertions and causal drivers of gains, is critical for a stronger, defensible narrative.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the requested scope (strengthening the text without drifting into unrelated tasks) maintains focus.
rationale.RX.4: Given potential policy impacts of climate narratives, avoiding downstream harm via misleading or overstated claims is essential.
rationale.RX.5: Clear provenance and traceability of evidence and sources are needed to support verifiability and trust.
